---
title: "Use Case 00001: Close Listening of Audio Recordings"
date: 2026-05-11
categories: [Use Case, Test]
tags: [audiovisual]     # TAG names should always be lowercase
---

**User Story:** As a humanities student, I want to annotate audio recordings so that I can present my close listening work as an assignment.

**Description:** Annotations can be added to an audio recording to capture and communicate the insights and analysis generated by close listening to the recording. Relevant fields include English, Social Studies, Film Studies, and Music and Sound Studies.

**Ingredients**
- 1 or more audio recordings
- Annotations
- Tags (optional)

**Method**
1. Perform close listening of audio recording(s), making note of observations and analyses with timestamps for all notes
  - Information found in this lesson plan can be used to better understand the concept of close listening
2. Upload audio recording(s) to AVAnnotate and add annotations, following Quick Start: How to use AVAnnotate guide
3. Create and add tags to annotations, if desired, following Quick Start guide

**Example:** [Reflexive Annotation of Oana Avasilichioaei's “Operator” Recordings](https://sarah-freeman.github.io/reflexive-annotation-/) by Sarah Freeman, and [Close Listening Assignment](https://jentery.github.io/SWDemo/output_documents/372.01_Winter_2021_Close_Listening_Assignment.html) by Jason Wiens (This is a lesson plan, not an AVAnnotate project, but could be used to generate an AVAnnotate project. This is part of Jentery Sayers’ SpokenWeb Pedagogy site - will seek permission to share more widely if included on a public use case website.)
